title: Modular Robots: Toward Field-capable, Intelligent Systems
subtitle: From reconfigurable hardware to robust autonomy: methods and systems for modular robots in real-world environments.
eventDateDisplay: September 27, 2026 (IROS workshop day, pending final conference schedule)
location: IROS 2026, Pittsburgh, PA, USA (room to be announced)
overview: This workshop addresses a central challenge in modular robotics: how can we move from impressive lab prototypes to reliable, field-capable systems? We focus on methods that tightly connect morphology, planning, control, perception, and learning under realistic uncertainty, hardware constraints, and deployment requirements.

focusAreas.title: Embodiment and Morphology
focusAreas.description: Design principles for self-reconfigurable structures, docking interfaces, and morphology-aware dynamics.

focusAreas.title: Planning and Control
focusAreas.description: Task-level and motion-level methods that adapt to changing robot topology and environment geometry.

focusAreas.title: Learning and Generalization
focusAreas.description: Policies and representations that transfer across module counts, layouts, and task families.

focusAreas.title: Systems and Deployment
focusAreas.description: Benchmarking, reproducibility, safety, and industrial or fielded case studies.

photos.alt: Modular robot blocks shown in a lab environment.
photos.title: Modular Robot Example
photos.description: Example of a modular robot platform demonstrating reconfigurable hardware concepts.

photos.alt: Conference scene from an IROS-related robotics event.
photos.title: IROS-Related Conference Scene
photos.description: Reference conference image for the IROS 2026 workshop page context.

cfpIntro: We invite contributions on theory, methods, and systems for modular robots, including both mature deployments and well-scoped early-stage ideas. We especially encourage submissions that clearly state assumptions, limitations, and expected real-world impact.
topics: Topology-aware motion planning and trajectory optimization
Composable low-level control and fault tolerance
Perception and state estimation for modular assemblies

submissionTypes.note: State problem, method, evaluation plan, and limitations.

submissionTypes.requirement: 1 page summary plus a 2-3 minute video link (optional but encouraged).
submissionTypes.note: Include hardware setup, floor-space requirements, and safety notes.
Identify open technical bottlenecks and concrete community directions.

reviewCriteria: Relevance to modular robotics and workshop scope
Novelty, insight, or practical deployment relevance
Quality of evidence, evaluation design, or system validation
Potential to stimulate cross-community discussion and collaboration

keyDates.label: Submission Site Opens
keyDates.note: Templates, topics, and submission instructions published.

keyDates.label: Early-Bird Submission Deadline
keyDates.note: Early decisions targeted before major registration deadlines.

keyDates.label: Regular Submission Deadline
keyDates.note: Poster and demo submissions remain welcome.

keyDates.label: Regular Notification
keyDates.note: Final acceptance decisions sent by email.

keyDates.label: Final Materials Due
keyDates.note: Final poster PDFs, demo details, and optional slides.

keyDates.label: Workshop Day
keyDates.note: Final room and schedule synchronized with IROS.

venueText: The final room assignment, visa-letter process, and travel details will be synchronized with the official IROS 2026 website. Program times are listed in EDT (UTC-4).
